item.title: Ex-DIG Jalil Mondal wants to become state’s evidence
item.description: The lawyer for detained former deputy inspector general of police Abdul Jalil Mondal on Wednesday told the International Crimes Tribunal-1 that his client was willing to become a state witness, subject to the tribunal’s permission, in a crimes against humanity case over the 2013 attacks on a Hefazat-e-Islam rally at Shapla Chattar in the capital......

item.title: Four killed in post-poll unrest in West Bengal
item.description: Four people have been killed inpoliticalunrest after Indian prime minister Narendra Modi’s Hindu-nationalist party celebrated victory in state polls in West Bengal, police and party officials said Wednesday......Politics

item.title: Rohingya man killed in shooting at Ukhiya camp
item.description: A member of the Rohingya community was killed on Wednesday afternoon in a shooting incident reportedly linked to rivalry over dominance at a Rohingya camp at Ukhiya upazila of Cox’s Bazar......

item.title: DMCH sets up 20-bed field hospital as measles surges
item.description: Dhaka Medical College Hospital has set up a 20-bed field hospital on its premises as measles cases continue surging across the country, putting additional pressure on the already overcrowded facility......

item.title: DMCH sets up 20-bed field hospital as measles surges
item.description: Dhaka Medical College Hospital has set up a 20-bed field hospital on its premises as measles cases continue surging across the country, putting additional pressure on the already overcrowded facility......

item.title: Govt to review post-uprising criminal cases: minister
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed on Wednesday said that the government sought district-wise information on cases filed during the post-July uprising interim government period for further review......

item.title: Army to return to barracks by end of June
item.description: The government has decided to withdraw members of the armed forces from the field and send them back to their respective barracks by the end of June this year, nearly two years after their deployment in July 2024......

item.title: PM calls for pro-people administration, hassle-free public services
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Wednesday called for building a people-oriented administration to ensure faster, transparent and harassment-free services for citizens......

item.title: ‘US, Iran closing in on one-page memo to end war’
item.description: The White House believes it’s getting close to an agreement with Iran on a one-page memorandum of understanding to end the war and set a framework for more detailed nuclear negotiations, according to two US officials and two other sources briefed on the issue, reports Axios on Wednesday......

item.title: US threatens new strikes, Iran reviews proposal to end war
item.description: US president Donald Trump threatened Iran on Wednesday with renewed bombing if it did not agree to a peace deal, piling on pressure after he abandoned an effort to reopen the Strait of Hormuz citing hopes for a breakthrough......

item.title: Three graveyards
item.description: STRATEGISTS have for generations invoked the image of Afghanistan as the ‘graveyard of empires,’ a land where foreign armies, from the British in the 19th century to the Americans in the 21st century, have seen their military ambitions crumble. But Afghanistan is not alone. Across Asia, two other nations have earned the epithets that speak to different forms of......

item.title: Do we consider those who feed us?
item.description: BANGLADESH has experienced visible economic expansion over the past decade, reflected in rising gross domestic product, growing corporate capital, expanding infrastructure, and an increasingly urbanised skyline. Average income levels and life expectancy have also improved on paper, signalling a degree of macroeconomic progress. Yet this aggregate picture sits......

item.title: Missing link in crisis management
item.description: Bangladesh today stands at a critical intersection of crises—public health, energy and climate-induced disasters. Individually, each exposes deep structural weaknesses. Together, they reveal a more troubling pattern: a persistent failure to learn, prepare and act early. As flash floods surge across haor regions and cyclone season looms, the question is no longer whether......

item.title: Cooling a city that no longer breathes
item.description: CITIES are not meant to hold heat the way a kiln does, yet many now behave as though they were built for that purpose. The phenomenon commonly described as the urban heat island is neither abstract nor distant; it is felt in the heavy stillness of late afternoons, in the absence of relief after sunset and in the growing strain on bodies and systems that were never......

item.title: Policy issues to deal with to address joblessness
item.description: THE recent International Labour Organisation report on global work force, learning opportunities and skills for future points to a number of issues that have held Bangladesh back from harnessing demographic dividend. The report, The World of Work Report: Lifelong Learning and Skills for the Future, shows that Bangladesh’s expanding work force increasingly outpaces......Country specific content

item.title: Public health facilities cannot be left idle
item.description: IT IS unfortunate that a technically ready health facility remains unused at a time when public health services struggle to care for suffering patients. The newly built 500-bed annexe to the National Institute of Neurosciences and Hospital in Dhaka has been non-operational since its inauguration in December 2025. The 15-storey facility, built at a cost of more than Tk 500 crore......

item.title: Holders PSG edge Bayern to reach Champions League final
item.description: Holders Paris Saint-Germain set up a Champions League final against Arsenal after a 1-1 second-leg draw at Bayern Munich secured a 6-5 aggregate victory on Wednesday....Local news updates

item.title: Uprising martyrs’ families for fair trial, rehabilitation
item.description: Family members of those killed in the July–August 2024 student-led mass uprising demanded on Wednesday a fair trial in the uprising massacre cases and the rehabilitation of the victims’ families......Trending news digests

item.title: Prosecution fails to prove ICT case against Inu: defence
item.description: Defence lawyers for Jatiya Samajtantrik Dal president Hasanul Haq Inu on Wednesday told the International Crimes Tribunal-1 that the prosecution had failed to prove the crimes against humanity case against their client......

item.title: DCs discuss ways to prevent hill cutting, forestland grabbing
item.description: Deputy commissioners on Wednesday discussed the measures that can be taken to prevent hill cutting and illegal sand extraction, recover encroached forest land and implement the government’s plan to plant 250 million trees over the next five years......

item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion has arrested 13 men following an attack on its intelligence team by suspected drug dealers on Tuesday, leaving three injured at Fatullah upazila in Narayanganj......

item.description: A Dhaka court on Wednesday set June 10 for delivering the verdict in a case filed against cricketer Nasir Hossain and his wife, Tamima Sultana Tammi, over allegations of adultery and fraud......Geographic Reference

item.description: Bangladesh Medical University on Wednesday held a workshop on PPA 2006, PPR 2025 and e-government procurement to ensure transparency and accountability in the procurement system......

item.title: Colonial states create permanent minorities: discussion
item.description: Researchers, teachers, and activists said on Wednesday that colonial states had created ‘permanent minorities’ through ‘identity politics’ and capitalism......

item.title: SSC examinee among three killed in road accidents
item.description: At least three people, including an SSC examinee and a five-year-old girl, were killed in road accidents in Noakhali, Dhaka and Gopalganj on Wednesday......Country specific content

item.description: Bangladesh Council of Scientific and Industrial Research on Wednesday signed service agreements with International Centre for Diarrhoeal Diseases Research, Bangladesh and leading pharmaceutical companies......

item.title: Several examiners unwilling to take SSC answer scripts
item.description: A number of examiners in the Dhaka division, appointed to assess answer scripts of the ongoing Secondary School Certificate examinations, have not collected the scripts due to low and delayed payment and communication gaps......

item.description: Continuous heavy rainfall and upstream flooding have caused widespread agricultural damage across the district, affecting nearly 50,000 farmers, according to the Department of Agricultural Extension......

item.title: HC questions EC decision cancelling Monira’s candidacy
item.description: The High Court on Wednesday questioned the legality of the Election Commission’s decision to cancel the candidacy of National Citizen Party joint convener Monira Sharmin for a reserved women’s seat in the 13th Parliament......

item.title: Van driver killed in clash between teen gangs in Rangpur
item.description: A van driver was hacked to death during a clash between two rival teenage gangs at Badarganj in Rangpur on Tuesday evening......

item.description: A three-year-old boy died after falling from the roof of a seven-storey building in the Bibirhat area of Fatikchari in Chattogram on Wednesday......

item.title: Hantavirus outbreak not like start of Covid: WHO
item.description: The WHO chief said on Wednesday he did not believe a deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship, which has sparked international alarm, has similarities with the start of the Covid pandemic......

item.title: Zelensky says Russia choosing war as dual ceasefires falter
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky said Wednesday that Russia had decided to reject efforts to halt fighting and save lives by launching fresh attacks on Ukraine, which had called a unilateral ceasefire......

item.description: At least five farmers were killed by lightning strikes in Lalmonirhat, Naogaon and Kurigram districts on Wednesday......

item.description: The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organisation has shown strong interest in launching a new initiative aimed at improving the social and mental well-being of university students in Bangladesh......
